headline: Agent infrastructure dominated the quarter as vector search and HNSW broke out alongside a wave of new entrants around control planes, elastic compute, and golden datasets.
body: Vector search and HNSW moved into breakout territory, matching a quarter defined by agent orchestration work: Google Cloud demonstrated 80 percent latency reductions through parallel execution, OpenTelemetry instrumentation, and profiling, and framed the Model Context Protocol as the discovery layer that lets agents chain tools and query vector stores. New entrants cluster around production plumbing, with control planes, elastic compute, retry mechanisms, golden datasets, and schema versioning entering the field. On the model side, Sonnet 5 landed to mixed reviews on pricing and coding performance, while OpenAI framed persistent agents as the future interface and raised safety concerns about self-sustaining research loops. Policy shifted too, as the White House imposed and then dropped a two-week restriction on Anthropic models.
